behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on the real vision model identifying explicit content, which a stub can mask. This is not just routing logic. Rejected higher level Workflow because the behavior describes one model judgment plus a hold/publish decision, not multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a curated set of explicit images to the real vision model path and assert each post is marked flagged, routed to human-review state, and absent from published feed/storage.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is model robustness to obfuscated hate speech; deterministic fakes would always pass and hide real misses. Rejected Workflow because this is a single text-model moderation decision, not a multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Send a labeled corpus of obfuscated hate-speech variants through the real text moderation model and assert they are flagged and held for review, with no publish event emitted.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is primarily cross-module handoff and state transition (image result + text result + publish path). The text says "passes both content checks," so model-quality judgment is preconditioned and can be represented by deterministic pass responses. Rejected Agentic because the behavior does not require validating model output quality, only orchestration after pass outcomes.
behaviors.test_description: Wire moderation orchestrator, persistence/queue, and publisher with deterministic model adapters returning PASS for both checks; assert a single publish record/event is created and no hold/drop terminal state occurs.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly measures an end-to-end sequence of multiple AI-backed steps (image check then text check then decision), so MULTI-STEP applies. Real infrastructure and real model calls both affect timing; lower levels can hide queueing/network/model-latency interactions.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end submissions through the real pipeline with real model endpoints enabled, measure submit-to-terminal-decision latency, and assert p95 (or specified SLA metric) is <=5s under peak-like load profile.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: The failure mode is outage behavior at a real external boundary (timeouts/5xx/connection failures). Pure mocks often miss transport/error-mapping realities that cause incorrect auto-approve paths. Rejected Agentic because correctness here is resilience/orchestration under unavailability, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Exercise the service against a real HTTP failure mode (e.g., unreachable endpoint, timeout, and 503) for vision checks and assert posts enter retry queue with backoff metadata and never transition to approved/published during outage window.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: This requires repeated real model-involved decisions over time plus pipeline state handling within a window, i.e., multiple agentic steps in sequence. A single-call or stubbed test cannot expose inconsistency across repeated submissions.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline image N times within the configured short window through the real moderation path and assert decision consistency rate meets threshold (or all identical if strict), including identical hold/publish terminal state each run.
